risks.description: The drawing/feature graph gives the formed radii and envelope but not the developed flat pattern or total blank cut perimeter. Exact blank development must be generated from CAD/tooling data before production blanking.
risks.mitigation: Generate CAD flat pattern using the 2.00 mm 1018 sheet bend allowance/K-factor, then validate with first-piece forming trial before running the 1000-piece batch.

risks.description: The part table has two rows and the starred bond-prep note may apply differently by part number. The route assumes the RFQ part CM03B364360 is made per AS SHOWN while retaining phosphate coating and bond-prep-all-around as drawing notes.
risks.mitigation: Confirm intended part-table row and whether PS-135.22 applies before releasing coating/bond-prep traveler.

risks.description: The 2X 5.5 A/C side-land callout and 2X 1.50 lower-land callout are geometrically ambiguous in the extracted drawing data.
risks.mitigation: Confirm feature interpretation against native CAD or customer drawing; incorporate confirmed details into blanking/forming die design.

feasibility.assessment_notes: Part is a small 2.00 mm SAE 1018 formed sheet plate with phosphate coating. Almity has in-house sheet metal stamping/forming, finish bench, coating/plating, and inspection capability. No heat treatment, grinding, holes, threads, or precision bores are present.

stock.material: SAE J1392 Grade 1018 steel sheet, 2.00 ±0.05 mm gage

starting_dimensions.notes: Use 2.00 ±0.05 mm sheet/coil. Per-part transverse width is 54.00 mm from SECTION A-A. Developed flat blank length and total cut perimeter are not explicitly provided and must come from CAD flat-pattern development/tool tryout.

stock.why: The natural stock form closest to the finished part is 2 mm sheet: the part is a formed arched plate with allowable thickness variation in formed areas, no machined bores or rotational features, and production quantity 1000. Sheet-metal blanking plus press/roll forming is preferred over machining from solid because it preserves sheet gage, minimizes waste, and matches Almity's in-house sheet metal stamping capability.

evidence.cited_text: METAL THICKNESS VARIATION +/-30% ALLOWABLE IN FORMED AREAS

route.route_name: Production route for 2 mm SAE 1018 formed arched rate plate with phosphate coating
route.route_reason: The part is sheet-metal dominated: 2.00 mm gage 1018 steel, formed R38.49 arched body, side lands/flanges, small formed corner radii, edge-break requirement, and phosphate coating. For 1000 pieces, use in-house blanking/forming tooling rather than machining from solid. Surface treatment is sequenced after all forming and deburring per coating rules.

setups.setup_name: Flat blank and form arched sheet plate
setups.machine_reason: The geometry is a thin 2.00 mm sheet part requiring blanking and a controlled 180-degree formed arch. Almity's sheet_metal_stamping capability supports blanking and forming with dedicated tooling, which is the most appropriate production method for quantity 1000.

workholding.grip_description: Sheet strip or nested sheet is located by die pilots/stops for flat blanking, then the blank is located in a radius forming die/roll-form fixture for the arched shape.
workholding.fixture_notes: Tool-room support is expected for blanking die, forming radius tooling, springback compensation, and a checking fixture. Exact flat pattern is pending CAD development.

datum_references: Use sheet gage faces as primary process reference during blanking
Use formed centerline and 54.00 mm transverse width as in-process inspection references
setups.stock_state_before: 2.00 ±0.05 mm SAE J1392 Grade 1018 sheet/strip, uncoated, nested for production blanking.
setups.stock_state_after: Uncoated formed arched plate with profile/land geometry created; forming burrs and sharp edges still present before deburr.

sub_operations.operation_name: Blank developed flat profile from 2.00 mm 1018 sheet
sub_operations.reason: Sheet-metal rules require all profile cutting/features that will be affected by forming to be made in the flat state before forming. This creates the developed outline, inner arcuate boundaries, lower end lands, and side land edges.

tool.tool_type: blanking die or production sheet cutting tool
tool.tool_description: Dedicated blanking die preferred for 1000-piece production; prototype/development blanks may be laser-cut or sheared if available before hard tooling.
tool.notes: Total cut perimeter is not available from the feature graph and must be filled from the developed flat pattern.

sub_operations.notes: Do not coat before blanking/forming. Keep burr direction consistent for later deburr.

sub_operations.operation_name: Press/roll form arched plate to outer R38.49 over 54.00 mm width
sub_operations.reason: Creates the main arched sheet body, R38.49 convex formed radius, concentric/parallel inner arcuate geometry, side lands/flanges, and R2.0 formed corner radii. Forming follows blanking so profile edges are already in the flat state.

tool.tool_description: Dedicated press-form or roll-form tooling for 2 mm mild-steel sheet, compensated for springback to achieve R38.49 outer radius and R2.0 side corner radii.
tool.notes: Trial forming required to validate springback and formed-area thickness variation.

sub_operations.notes: Treat the arch as one continuous forming operation rather than discrete press-brake bends.

sub_operations.operation_name: First-piece check of formed radius, width, thickness, and land features
sub_operations.reason: Validate forming die setup, springback compensation, 54.00 mm transverse width, 2.00 mm gage, R38.49/R32.30 radii, R2.0 corners, and small land features before producing the batch.

tool.tool_description: Radius templates, formed-profile go/no-go gauge, calipers, and sheet-thickness micrometer.
tool.notes: Inspection check fixture should reference the same process centerline used in forming.

sub_operations.notes: If first-piece results miss R38.49 or 54.00 mm width, adjust tooling/springback compensation before batch run.

setups.inspection_at_setup: First-piece dimensional check of formed radius, transverse width, sheet thickness, R2.0 corners, and land features.
setups.setup_reason: Blanking/forming must precede edge break and coating. For volume 1000, a dedicated stamping/forming setup minimizes per-piece handling and maintains repeatability.
assumptions: Developed flat blank is generated from CAD/tooling development, not directly from the raster drawing.
One continuous 180-degree forming operation represents the arched radius.
No piercing/hole operations are required because no holes or slots were extracted.

setups.setup_name: Manual deburr and edge break before coating
setups.machine_reason: The drawing requires all sharp edges broken 0.5 max, and surface-treatment rules require broken edges before coating to avoid thin/peeling coating. Manual/tumble deburr at finish bench is appropriate for small 2 mm sheet parts.

workholding.method: Bench nest, soft jaws, or light tumble/deburr basket
workholding.grip_description: Hold formed plate in a soft nest to avoid flattening the arch while operators break exposed blanked edges and corners.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use formed support nest if hand deburring causes distortion.

datum_references: Use visual/all-around edge access rather than datum-controlled machining
setups.stock_state_before: Uncoated formed arched plate with blanking/forming burrs.
setups.stock_state_after: Uncoated formed arched plate with all sharp edges broken to 0.5 mm maximum and suitable for phosphate coating.

sub_operations.operation_name: Break all sharp edges 0.5 mm maximum
sub_operations.reason: Drawing explicitly requires all sharp edges broken 0.5 max. This must occur before phosphate coating because sharp edges produce poor coating adhesion and thin coverage.

tool.tool_type: manual deburr tools or vibratory finishing media
tool.tool_description: Fine file, abrasive wheel, Scotch-Brite wheel/pad, or controlled tumble media suitable for 2 mm mild-steel sheet.
tool.notes: Avoid flattening or distorting the formed arch during deburr.

sub_operations.notes: Maintain edge break at or below 0.5 mm maximum; target a consistent small break adequate for coating adhesion.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual/tactile check that all blanked and formed sharp edges are broken without over-rounding.
setups.setup_reason: Deburr is sequenced after forming and before phosphate coating to satisfy both drawing edge-break and surface-treatment key rules.
assumptions: Edge break is applied all around, including lower lands, side lands, and arched edges.

setups.setup_name: Clean, bond prep, and phosphate coat
setups.machine_reason: The drawing specifies phosphate coat per PS-119.0 and bond prep all around. Almity has an in-house coating/plating line; surface treatment must occur after all forming and edge break operations.

workholding.grip_description: Rack or basket small formed plates to expose all surfaces for cleaning, bond prep, and phosphate coating without nesting contact marks on significant surfaces.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use racks that avoid damaging the 2 mm formed arch and allow all-around solution flow.

datum_references: All surfaces are treated unless customer identifies mask/no-coat zones
setups.stock_state_before: Deburred uncoated formed plate, free of sharp edges and ready for chemical cleaning.
setups.stock_state_after: Cleaned, bond-prepped, phosphate-coated, rinsed, and dried formed plate.

sub_operations.operation_name: Clean, degrease, and bond-prep all surfaces
sub_operations.reason: Surface-treatment sequence requires all oil, burr debris, and contamination removed before coating. The drawing note also calls for bond prep all around part.

tool.tool_description: Cleaning tanks, rinse stations, and bond-prep chemistry compatible with SAE 1018 steel and the applicable customer process specification.
tool.notes: Verify water-break cleanliness before phosphate coating.

sub_operations.notes: If the starred bond-prep note is confirmed not applicable to CM03B364360, this operation can be limited to coating pre-clean per PS-119.0.

sub_operations.operation_name: Apply phosphate coating per PS-119.0 all around
sub_operations.reason: Drawing part-level process note specifies phosphate coating. Coating is performed only after all forming, deburr, and cleaning operations are complete.

tool.tool_type: phosphate conversion coating bath and racks
tool.tool_description: Phosphate coating process equipment suitable for carbon steel per PS-119.0 or approved equivalent internal process.
tool.notes: Masking is not planned because no threads, bores, or precision mating no-coat zones were extracted.

sub_operations.notes: Hydrogen embrittlement bake is not expected for low-carbon 1018 at 340 MPa yield, unless PS-119.0 or customer specification requires a bake.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rinse and dry after phosphate treatment
sub_operations.reason: Removes residual chemistry and leaves the phosphate-coated part dry and stable for final inspection.

tool.tool_description: Clean rinse followed by air/oven drying compatible with phosphate-coated mild steel.
tool.notes: Dry without water spotting or contact damage.

sub_operations.notes: Do not perform any subsequent machining after coating.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Water-break cleanliness check before coating and visual coverage check after coating.
setups.setup_reason: Surface treatment is placed after all cutting, forming, and edge break operations, satisfying the coating sequence rule that all dimensional work must be complete before treatment.
assumptions: Phosphate coating per PS-119.0 can be performed using Almity's in-house coating/plating capability or an approved internal equivalent.
No masking is required because no threads, precision bores, or specified no-coat zones were extracted.

setups.machine_reason: Final acceptance requires dimensional checks of the formed sheet profile and visual/coating checks. Almity has in-house dimensional and visual inspection capability suitable for the specified general tolerances.

workholding.method: Inspection bench and formed check fixture
workholding.grip_description: Place coated formed part in a soft support/check fixture that references the formed profile and transverse width without scratching coating.
workholding.fixture_notes: A simple go/no-go nest is recommended for the R38.49 arch and 54.00 mm width at production quantity 1000.

datum_references: Inspect to drawing dimensions using formed-profile centerline and section width references
setups.stock_state_after: Inspected accepted part ready for packing/shipping.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final dimensional inspection of formed sheet profile
sub_operations.reason: Verify the drawing-defined geometry after forming and coating: 2.00 mm gage, 54.00 mm width, R38.49 outer crown, 2X R32.30 inner arcs, 2X 1.50 lower lands, 2X 5.5 side lands, and R2.0 side corner radii.

tool.tool_description: Formed-profile go/no-go fixture, radius gauges/templates, calipers, height gauge, and thickness micrometer.
tool.notes: CMM not required by drawing; use outside CMM only if customer requests FAI beyond in-house capability.

sub_operations.notes: Pay special attention to the low-confidence F005 side-land interpretation during inspection planning.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final visual, coating, and edge-break inspection
sub_operations.reason: Confirm phosphate coverage/appearance, all-around bond-prep/coating condition, absence of coating defects, and compliance with the 0.5 mm maximum edge-break requirement before release.

tool.tool_description: Visual inspection lighting, edge-break comparator, coating appearance/thickness tools as required by PS-119.0, and witness coupon checks if specified.
tool.notes: If PS-119.0 mandates adhesion or salt-spray coupon testing, add test-bench/vendor validation per customer requirement.

sub_operations.notes: Final inspection occurs after coating; do not rework coated edges except through approved strip/recoat process.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Final dimensional, visual, coating, and edge-break release inspection.
setups.setup_reason: Final inspection is last, after all forming, deburr, cleaning, and coating are complete.
assumptions: In-house inspection is adequate for general sheet-metal tolerances; no CMM/FAI requirement was extracted.
Coating acceptance criteria follow PS-119.0 or customer standard if supplied.

routing_notes: Applicable route families used: sheet_metal for blank/form/deburr and surface_treatment for phosphate coating. The pre-loaded milling route was evaluated but not used as a primary route because there are no machined datum faces, holes, pockets, slots, or prismatic stock-removal features.
All cutting/blanking is placed before forming to avoid feature distortion and to satisfy sheet-metal sequencing rules.
Deburr/edge break is placed before coating to satisfy both the drawing note and the surface-treatment rule for coating adhesion.
All dimensional forming and edge finishing are complete before phosphate coating; no machining is planned after coating.
No heat treatment is specified. Hydrogen embrittlement bake is not expected for SAE 1018 at the stated 340 MPa minimum yield unless PS-119.0 requires it.
No holes, threads, press-in hardware, welds, or masking zones were identified; those route steps are intentionally omitted.

For quantity 1000, use dedicated blanking/forming tooling and a simple inspection nest/check fixture. Prototype or tool-prove blanks may be made by alternate flat cutting if needed before production tooling.

clarifications.question: Please provide the native CAD flat pattern, developed blank drawing, or approved K-factor/bend allowance for the 2.00 mm 1018 formed arch.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The sheet-metal blanking step requires total cut perimeter and developed blank length. The feature graph provides formed radii and envelope but not the flat blank geometry, so cut_perimeter_mm is intentionally left null.
clarifications.suggested_default: Generate flat pattern from CAD using 2.00 mm mild-steel tooling data, then validate by first-piece forming trial before production.

clarifications.question: Which part-table row is the RFQ intended to cover: CM03B364360 with process per AS SHOWN, or CM03B364361 with PS-135.22 and the starred bond-prep requirement?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The route currently assumes CM03B364360 / AS SHOWN while retaining phosphate coating and bond prep all around. If PS-135.22 applies, the coating/bond-prep traveler may need additional process controls.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use CM03B364360 / AS SHOWN matching the RFQ file name; keep phosphate coat per PS-119.0 and bond-prep-all-around unless customer says otherwise.

clarifications.question: Confirm the intended interpretation of SECTION A-A callout '2X 5.5 A/C' and main-view '2X 1.50': are these side land/as-cut offsets and lower end land heights respectively?
clarifications.why_it_matters: These features drive the blanking die/profile and final inspection fixture. The raster extraction flagged this geometry as ambiguous.
clarifications.suggested_default: Interpret 2X 5.5 A/C as two 5.5 mm side land offsets in SECTION A-A and 2X 1.50 as two lower end straight land heights.

clarifications.question: Confirm the required phosphate coating process details for PS-119.0, including coating type/class, thickness or weight, post-treatment oil/seal, and inspection/test requirements.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The route can sequence the coating, but acceptance criteria and any required tests depend on the customer specification details not present in the feature graph.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as an in-house phosphate conversion coating on carbon steel with cleaning, water-break verification, coating, rinse/dry, and visual/coating inspection per PS-119.0.

assumptions.text: Production quantity is 1000 pieces as provided in the production context, overriding the null quantity in the feature graph.
SAE J1392 Grade 1018 is treated as formable low-carbon/mild steel sheet supplied at 2.00 ±0.05 mm gage, with no heat treatment required.
The R38.49 arch is treated as one continuous press/roll forming operation over the 54.00 mm transverse width, rather than multiple discrete press-brake bends.
No holes, threads, pressed-in hardware, welds, precision bores, or no-coat masking zones exist because none were extracted in the feature graph.
All setup_time_min values are set to 0.0 as placeholders because this agent is not estimating setup time or cost; time enrichment should be performed by the downstream post-processor/shop estimator.
Phosphate coating per PS-119.0 can be handled by Almity's in-house coating/plating capability unless the customer specification mandates a special external-approved process.

analysis_trace.summary: The part is a small formed arched sheet plate made from 2.00 ±0.05 mm SAE J1392 Grade 1018 steel. Major features are the R38.49 formed outer arch, 2X R32.30 inner arcuate features, two lower end lands, side lands/flanges, R2.0 side corner radii, and all-around 0.5 max edge break. No holes, threads, slots, welds, or formal datums were identified. Phosphate coating and bond prep are process notes.
issues: Flat pattern and developed cut perimeter are not provided.
F004/F005 small land callouts are ambiguous.

analysis_trace.summary: The applicable library routes are sheet_metal and surface_treatment. Sheet metal applies because the part is 2 mm gage formed sheet. Surface treatment applies because phosphate coating is specified. Milling was considered from the pre-loaded route but rejected as primary production routing because the feature graph contains no machined prismatic features or holes.
issues: Sheet-metal route requires cut_perimeter_mm for blanking, but the developed profile is missing.

analysis_trace.summary: Classified as part_family=sheet_metal and base_geometry=complex formed arched sheet plate. The dominant operations are blanking, forming, deburr, phosphate coating, and inspection.

Organizational routing corrections were searched for sheet_metal/complex/mild_steel with formed sheet, arched profile, edge break, and phosphate coating at volume 1000. No matching corrections were returned.

Selected SAE J1392 Grade 1018 sheet at 2.00 ±0.05 mm gage. Sheet stock is closest to the finished geometry, preserves the required gage, and avoids machining waste. Per-part blank transverse width uses 54.00 mm; developed length/perimeter remains a clarification item.
issues: Stock flat blank length cannot be populated without flat pattern/K-factor.

analysis_trace.summary: Built the route as: blank flat profile, form arch, first-piece check, deburr/edge break, clean/bond prep, phosphate coat, rinse/dry, final inspection. Key rules satisfied: all blanking before forming, deburr before coating, all dimensional work before surface treatment, coating inspection last.
issues: Phosphate specification details and bond-prep applicability require confirmation.

analysis_trace.summary: Created four machine setups using only valid shop machines: sheet_metal_stamping, finish_bench, plating_line, and inspection. Sub-operations are atomic and target existing feature IDs. Formula inputs use available geometry such as sheet_thickness_mm=2.0, bend_length_mm=54.0, external_diameter_mm=76.98, and chamfer_size_mm=0.5; unavailable cut_perimeter_mm is left null with a clarification.
issues: Some inspection/forming fields use the closest available standardized formula parameters because the registry lacks explicit bend radius/angle fields.

analysis_trace.summary: All extracted features F001-F007 are covered by at least one sub-operation. Every sub-operation has at least one non-null geometric formula input. Stock states progress logically from sheet to formed, deburred, coated, and inspected part. Workholding is feasible with dedicated forming tooling and soft support nests. Surface treatment key rules are satisfied.
issues: Final production release depends on customer confirmation of ambiguous callouts and coating/bond-prep specification details.
